1.texto_original: Y vino a mí palabra de Jehová, diciendo:
1.normal:
Devotional Study of Ezequiel 34:1

"Y vino a mí palabra de Jehová, diciendo:"

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Ezequiel 34:1

Philological Analysis

"Y vino a mí palabra de Jehová, diciendo:"

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Y vino a mí palabra de Jehová, diciendo:"

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

2.texto_original: Hijo de hombre, profetiza contra los pastores de Israel; profetiza, y diles a los pastores: Así dice Jehová el Señor: ¡Ay de los pastores de Israel, que se apacientan a sí mismos! ¿No deben los pastores apacentar los rebaños?

8.texto_original: Vivo yo, dice Jehová el Señor, que por cuanto mi rebaño ha venido a ser por presa, y por falta de pastor mis ovejas han venido a ser por comida a todas las fieras del campo; y mis pastores no buscaron mis ovejas, sino que los pastores se apacentaron a sí mismos, y no apacentaron mis ovejas;

10.texto_original: Así dice Jehová el Señor: He aquí, yo [estoy] contra los pastores; y requeriré mis ovejas de su mano, y les haré dejar de apacentar las ovejas; ni los pastores se apacentarán más a sí mismos; pues yo libraré mis ovejas de sus bocas, y no les serán más por comida.

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
28.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Y no serán más por presa a las naciones, ni las fieras de la tierra los devorarán; sino que habitarán seguros, y no habrá quien [los] espante."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

29.texto_original: Y levantaré para ellos una planta de renombre, y no serán ya más consumidos de hambre en la tierra, ni ya más serán avergonzados por las naciones.
29.normal:
Devotional Study of Ezequiel 34:29

"Y levantaré para ellos una planta de renombre, y no serán ya más consumidos de hambre en la tierra, ni ya más serán avergonzados por las naciones."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
